title: Women can sign a contract on the same terms as men
subtitle: Women can sign contracts on their own, without permission or signatures from a husband or guardian.
caption:
Women can sign a contract on the same terms as men

Women can sign contracts on their own, without permission or signatures from a husband or guardian.

This visualization presents two choropleth world maps comparing the legal status of women signing contracts in 1970 and 2023. The legend defines three categories: "No data" indicated by diagonal hatching, "No" represented by orange, and "Yes" represented by blue. In the 1970 map, the majority of the world, including North America, Europe, and the Soviet Union, is colored blue, indicating that women could sign contracts on the same terms as men. However, distinct orange areas show restrictions in place for several regions. In South America, Argentina and Paraguay appear in orange. In Africa, a cluster of nations including South Africa, Botswana, Zimbabwe, Mozambique, and Tanzania are colored orange, alongside Morocco in North Africa. In Asia and Oceania, Indonesia, Papua New Guinea, and Spain are also shown in orange. Greenland displays the hatched pattern for no data. By 2023, the map on the right shows a uniform shift to blue across all previously orange regions. Argentina, the Southern African nations, Indonesia, and Spain now match the blue color of the rest of the mapped world, indicating that the legal restriction denoted by the orange band has been removed in these locations. The hatched area over Greenland remains unchanged between the two years. The visual comparison highlights a global convergence toward the "Yes" category over the fifty-three-year period.
